Title: GAME9 regulates the biosynthesis of steroidal alkaloids and upstream isoprenoids in the plant mevalonate pathway

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sterol desaturase; ERG3-like C5 sterol desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing in tomato; GC-MS sterol profiling; LC-MS α-tomatine quantification; yeast erg3 complementation assay; promoter transactivation assay; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq transcriptome analysis in tomato GAME9-Ox and GAME9-RNAi lines and potato GAME9-Ox lines; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth at 24 °C day/18 °C night, natural light; leaf and green fruit tissues analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GAME9 in tomato; upregulated in GAME9-Ox and downregulated in GAME9-RNAi backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf and green fruit expression reduced in VIGS-silenced tomato plants; expression altered according to GAME9 levels in tomato and increased in potato GAME9-Ox
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Steroidal glycoalkaloids contribute to pathogen defense but are anti-nutritional/toxic to humans
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C5-SD was identified as a GAME9-responsive cholesterol-pathway gene and a direct promoter target of GAME9. 2. Silencing C5-SD reduced cholesterol and α-tomatine and caused accumulation of cholesta-7-enol, supporting its role in cholesterol biosynthesis. 3. Tomato C5-SD complemented the yeast erg3 mutant, confirming C5(6)-desaturase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reduce steroidal glycoalkaloid accumulation by limiting cholesterol supply; may be combined with GAME9 manipulation for metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely catalyzes a non-alkylated sterol branch step; authors speculate a different paralog may catalyze the analogous step in C-24 alkylsterol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing increased cycloartenol and C-24 alkylsterol intermediates and decreased β-amyrin, indicating broader sterol/triterpene perturbation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cholesterol formation and thereby supports α-tomatine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; identified as a key cholesterol-pathway step upstream of SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cholesterol biosynthesis / steroidal glycoalkaloid precursor pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream, non-alkylated sterol branch upstream of cholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cholesta-7-enol; episterol in yeast complementation context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a C5-C6 double bond into Δ7 sterol substrates, likely converting cholesta-7-enol to 7-dehydrocholesterol in the plant cholesterol pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an intermediate required for cholesterol production, which supplies precursor flux to steroidal glycoalkaloids

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ERF IXa subfamily AP2/ERF; serine-rich C-terminal domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in tomato and potato; RNAi silencing in tomato; qRT-PCR; RNA-Seq; LC-MS SGA profiling; GC-MS sterol profiling; transient promoter transactivation assays; EMSA; co-expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq transcriptome analysis in tomato GAME9-Ox and GAME9-RNAi lines and potato GAME9-Ox lines; co-expression analysis using tomato and potato RNA-Seq datasets; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth at 24 °C day/18 °C night with natural light; tissues analyzed include leaves, flower buds, fruits, tuber peel; QTL noted under dark and light-exposed potato tubers from prior study
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In tomato, highly expressed in leaf and flower buds, early fruit stages, vascular tissue and outer epidermis of leaf blade; in potato co-expressed with SGA genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: C5-SD; HMGR1; GAME4; GAME7; multiple SGA biosynthetic GAME genes; SSR2 and CAS expression affected in planta
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Steroidal glycoalkaloids contribute to pathogen defense but are anti-nutritional/toxic to humans
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME9 is the central AP2/ERF regulator of steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis in tomato and potato. 2. GAME9 overexpression increases major SGAs, while tomato GAME9 silencing strongly decreases them. 3. GAME9 also controls upstream mevalonate/cholesterol pathway genes and directly targets C5-SD, acting partly cooperatively with SlMYC2.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major leverage point for modulating anti-nutritional SGAs in Solanaceae crops; suitable for marker-assisted breeding, editing, or transgene stacking with pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in an ERF gene cluster on chromosome 1 in tomato and potato; likely key regulator though functional redundancy with nearby GAME9-like ERFs cannot be excluded
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber peel and leaves; tomato leaves and green fruit
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alters multiple upstream sterol and triterpene branches including campesterol, β-sitosterol, cholesterol, cycloartenol, cycloartanol and β-amyrin, indicating broader mevalonate-pathway perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of major steroidal glycoalkaloids and modifies upstream sterol/isoprenoid intermediates
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator coordinating SGA biosynthesis together with upstream cholesterol/mevalonate precursor supply
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Likely jasmonate-related via SlMYC2 analogy; direct upstream signal not experimentally established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tomato leaf and flower buds, immature fruit, vascular tissues and outer leaf epidermis; transcript increased in GAME9-Ox and reduced in RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and transactivation effectors
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedback from SGA or cholesterol intermediates on upper mevalonate genes is discussed but not established
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates C5-SD directly; synergistically activates C5-SD, HMGR1, GAME4 and GAME7 with SlMYC2; positively regulates multiple SGA/cholesterol genes in planta
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; cooperative promoter transactivation with bHLH factor SlMYC2; direct promoter binding to GCC-rich motifs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of steroidal glycoalkaloids and control of upstream mevalonate/cholesterol precursor supply
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts cooperatively with SlMYC2 on shared target promoters; direct physical complex not shown

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient promoter transactivation assays in tobacco protoplasts; EMSA DNA-binding assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in tobacco BY-2 protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Steroidal glycoalkaloids contribute to pathogen defense but are anti-nutritional/toxic to humans
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Tobacco protoplast assay system; tomato biological pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYC2 alone transactivated the C5-SD promoter and mildly activated the GAME4 promoter. 2. SlMYC2 synergized with GAME9 to transactivate C5-SD, HMGR1, GAME4 and GAME7 promoters. 3. SlMYC2 bound the C5-SD promoter G-box in EMSA.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering target with GAME9 to tune SGA and precursor-pathway transcriptional output
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a mechanistic co-regulator in Results, but not the primary central gene of the study
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato leaves and green fruit in pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect broader jasmonate-responsive metabolism, but not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances transcription of selected cholesterol/mevalonate/SGA genes, especially in combination with GAME9
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent cooperative activator that boosts GAME9-mediated transcription of SGA-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signaling context inferred from MYC2 identity and discussion, not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in effector constructs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates C5-SD and mildly GAME4 alone; synergistically activates C5-SD, HMGR1, GAME4 and GAME7 with GAME9
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation through G-box binding; cooperative co-transactivation with GAME9
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of cholesterol/mevalonate/SGA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Cooperative action with GAME9 on target promoters; direct physical complex not demonstrated
